title: Implement typed Heimlern outcome consumer smoke
goal: Implement a dedicated, review-only Heimlern consumer for the merged Chronik operator-outcome envelope; validate both contract layers and emit an auditable report or bounded proposal without applying policy.

acceptance.assertion: A dedicated consumer parses chronik.operator-routing-outcome-export.v1 rather than routing the artifact through the unrelated generic AussenEvent path.
The consumer validates envelope identity, pinned Heimlern payload contract revision/digest, canonical payload digest, deterministic event id and evidence-reference digests before analysis.
Freshness is recomputed at read time with an explicit policy; duplicate event ids are handled deterministically without double-counting evidence.
The smoke emits a bounded report, a policy.weight_adjustment.v1 proposal that validates, or a typed insufficient-evidence result.
No routing policy, live weights, Chronik history or Grabowski execution state is mutated; no-auto-apply non-claims survive the output.
Focused tests cover valid input, contract drift, payload/event digest mismatch, stale input, duplicate input, secret/raw-field rejection and insufficient evidence.

metadata.source_decision: Chronik selected as primary integration organ for heimlern because it owns append-only historical outcomes.
metadata.scope_basis: T001 live audit plus merged Chronik PR #214.
metadata.consumer_gap: The existing Heimlern Chronik CLI consumes AussenEvent and must not be reused as an implicit routing-outcome parser.
